category_title: At-risk students in the Normal Technical stream exhibiting attention-seeking or disengaged behaviors
minimal_context: The student is in a keyboard lab trying to learn chords C, F, G, and A using a handout and patterns written on the board.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Please look at the handout showing the notes and the patterns I wrote on the board. We are going to practice playing the chords C, F, G, and A.

STUDENT: (Touches the keyboard immediately) I want to try it, but I'm having a hard time following the chord changes.

TUTOR: Just keep trying to follow the pattern. I am going to step away for a minute.

STUDENT: (Keeps pressing keys while losing focus) I can't get the changes right.

TUTOR: Keep practicing the chords on your handout.

STUDENT: (Cannot stop himself from touching the keyboard and decides to play the keyboard really loudly, becoming very disruptive)

options.A: Provide explicit guidelines, expectations, and goals for the keyboard lab activity during the rest of the session.
options.B: Assign keyboard activities the student has already mastered, as opposed to introducing novel chord assignments.
options.C: Immediately present the student with a visual box notation for the chords to use during the keyboard activity.
options.D: Use a 'Ready to Learn' thermometer to emphasize the relationship between the student's thoughts, emotions, and behavior.
